title: Syzygium obliquinervium
taxonomy: Syzygium obliquinervium is classified in the family Myrtaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. This family includes many species of trees and shrubs that are native to the tropics and subtropics around the world. Syzygium obliquinervium is also classified in the genus Syzygium, which includes a wide range of species that are commonly known as water apple or Davidson's plum. The genus Syzygium is further divided into several sections, each of which includes a group of closely related species. Within the family Myrtaceae, Syzygium obliquinervium is part of the tribe Syzygieae, which is a group of flowering plants that are characterized by their showy flowers and edible fruit.

intro: Syzygium obliquinervium, also known as the tropical apple or rose apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and combating infections. The bioactive constituents of Syzygium obliquinervium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Syzygium obliquinervium include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
